sourceMovie.title: Shaolin
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Shaolin works as a action movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The action and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Crouching Tiger, Hidden Dragon
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Shaolin clicked for you, Crouching Tiger, Hidden Dragon (2000) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and drama, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Bloodsport
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Shaolin clicked for you, Bloodsport (1988) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Shaolin?
faq.answer: Try Crouching Tiger, Hidden Dragon, Bloodsport, Frontier(s)—each shares DNA with Shaolin while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Shaolin?
faq.answer: Shaolin (2011) is best described as Action, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Shaolin worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
